mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a strengthening labor market and broader economic recovery, which typically boosts investor confidence and drives S&P 500 forward returns higher as corporate earnings improve.
evidence_refs: fact:historical correlation between employment and equity markets
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns for the S&P 500 are predominantly negative (e.g., >55% negative) during periods of sustained UNRATE decline, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: An extremely low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates an accommodative monetary policy stance by the central bank, which reduces the cost of capital and makes equities more attractive relative to fixed income, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:central bank easing supports asset prices
regime_hint: Zero Lower Bound (ZLB) or near-ZLB environment
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of instances when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) combined with a falling unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals robust consumer demand and a healthy labor market, which collectively drive corporate revenue and profit growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of periods where both RSAFS and UNRATE conditions are met, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) during a recovery phase indicates increasing manufacturing activity and capacity utilization, signaling improving corporate fundamentals and driving S&P 500 forward returns higher.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in over 45% of instances when INDPRO shows growth during post-recession periods, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained low inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, provides the central bank with flexibility to maintain accommodative monetary policies, which keeps discount rates low and supports higher equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:low inflation allows for lower interest rates
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of periods where CPIAUCSL 12-month growth is below 2.0%, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: An increasing trend in housing starts (HOUST) reflects improving consumer and business confidence, indicating broader economic strength and investment, which typically translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:housing market as a leading economic indicator
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 5-day forward returns show a statistically insignificant or negative correlation with the HOUST trend over a 3-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Strong employment growth (PAYEMS) coupled with robust GDP growth (GDPC1) signifies a healthy and expanding economy, leading to increased corporate profits and higher stock prices, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change > 100k AND GDPC1 1-quarter growth > 2.0% annualized
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of periods where both PAYEMS and GDPC1 conditions are met, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in retail sales (RSAFS) signals weakening consumer demand and potential recessionary pressures, leading to lower corporate earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 forward returns as investors price in a slowdown.
evidence_refs: fact:consumer spending is a major component of GDP
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal from RSAFS, the hypothesis is rejected.
